summary: The Goshen Township Board of Trustees held a regular meeting covering road paving grants, cemetery error resolution, fire department equipment, and various personnel matters. A major OPWC grant of $495,540 was accepted for repaving Smith Road, Bay Road, and Charles Snyder Road. The board also addressed selling a temporary manufactured home from Station 18, approved a traffic study request, and handled donations and equipment matters for the fire department.
decisions: Motion to adopt the agenda passed unanimously
Motion to approve payment of bills (June 9 - July 14, 2026) passed unanimously
Motion to accept OPWC grant of $495,540 for repaving Smith Road, Bay Road, and Charles Snyder Road passed unanimously
Motion to approve Resolution R024-2026 establishing Tax Incentive Review Council with Trustee Allen and Administrator Rose as representatives passed unanimously
Motion to approve Resolution R025-2026 authorizing settlement agreement for cemetery internment error correction passed unanimously
Motion to request Clermont County engineer to conduct traffic study at Cross Creek Drive and Pine Meadows Drive intersection passed unanimously
Motion to approve out-of-town travel for Officer Tyler Smith for pistol mounted optics training passed unanimously
Motion to approve out-of-town travel for Detective Wesley Lyons for police rifle carbine instructor training ($700 registration) passed unanimously
Motion to consult with legal and if authorized sell manufactured home to Route 73 Sales for $25,000 passed unanimously
Motion to accept resignation of part-time firefighter EMT Phillip Cruz effective June 23, 2026 passed unanimously
Motion to accept donation of three $100 gift cards from CPAA passed unanimously
Motion to accept donation of four $25 Domino's Pizza gift cards from Miss Pam's Child Care passed unanimously
Motion to declare 16 mattresses obsolete and donate to Goshen Community Resource Center passed unanimously
Motion to destroy 16 obsolete Panasonic mobile data computers passed unanimously
Motion to approve additional touchscreen for officer's side of Quint 19 at $6,000 passed unanimously
Motion to enter executive session under ORC 121.22G1 passed unanimously
Motion to approve salary increase for service worker Chris Helton to $24.72/hour effective upon completion of probationary period (July 28, 2026) passed unanimously
Motion to adjourn at 6:59 passed unanimously
action_items: Chief Myers to consult with legal counsel on legitimacy of direct sale of manufactured home to Route 73 Sales for $25,000
Chief Myers to proceed with sale of manufactured home to Route 73 Sales if legally authorized
Administrator Rose to sign OPWC grant-related documents for road repaving project
Trustee Allen and Administrator Rose to attend Tax Incentive Review Council meeting on August 5
County engineer to conduct traffic study at Cross Creek Drive and Pine Meadows Drive intersection
Director Meadows/Chad to coordinate road repaving with Clermont County
Director Meadows to go through cemetery records A through ZZ to ensure no duplicate grave sales exist
Chief Myers to arrange for Sutphen to install additional touchscreen on Quint 19 at the plant
key_amounts: $495,540 OPWC grant for repaving Smith Road, Bay Road, and Charles Snyder Road
$631,000 total project price for road repaving (79% grant, 21% local match)
$116.36 per ton for salt (up from $92.75 last year)
$25,000 direct sale price offered by Route 73 Sales for manufactured home
$81,000 original value of manufactured home when new
$700 registration cost for Detective Lyons rifle carbine instructor training
$24.72 per hour new rate for service worker Chris Helton
Nearly $700,000 in total grants secured by grants coordinator Donna Rossic in under three years
$300 in gift cards donated by CPAA (three $100 cards)
$100 in gift cards donated by Miss Pam's Child Care (four $25 Domino's cards)
notable_topics: OPWC grant acceptance for 3.32 miles of road repaving
Salt bid increase from $92.75 to $116.36 per ton
Cemetery internment error correction - graves double-sold in 2006 and 2013
Sale of temporary Station 18 manufactured home
Traffic study request for Timber Ridge subdivision intersection
Police officer training approvals (pistol optics and rifle carbine instructor)
Disposal of obsolete Panasonic mobile data computers
Donation of 16 mattresses to Goshen Community Resource Center
Eagles Nest Final Development Plan coming through for review
Clermont County Fair announcements including new drone show
Extreme heat conditions affecting service workers
Medic unit in Columbus delayed on parts
Resignation of part-time firefighter EMT Phillip Cruz
Tax Incentive Review Council annual appointment
Chris Helton salary increase upon probation completion
notes: The cemetery double-sale error was a unique and sensitive situation where the same graves were sold to two different families in 2006 and 2013, requiring disinterment and relocation. The manufactured home was originally paid for by insurance during a temporary station situation. The fire department received 16 mattresses instead of 8 mattresses and 8 box springs due to a vendor error, which were then donated. Trustees noted that fiscal restraint in annual paving budgets allowed them to build reserves for grant matching opportunities. The meeting included an executive session regarding employee compensation.
title: BOT 7-14-26
